description_long: This object is a flintlock-style pistol with a classic, historical design, featuring a wooden grip and a metallic barrel. The barrel is relatively short and flares outward at the muzzle, typical of blunderbuss pistols from the 18th century. The lock mechanism, hammer, and trigger assembly are prominently visible, with the hammer cocked back and the frizzen in place. The grip is smoothly contoured and appears to be made of polished wood, while the trigger guard is metallic and slightly curved. The overall finish is a combination of matte wood and smooth, slightly reflective metal. There are no visible engravings or markings on the barrel or slide, and the magazine base is not externally visible, as this is a single-shot, muzzle-loading firearm.
description: A historical flintlock pistol with a wooden grip, flared metal barrel, and classic trigger mechanism. The design is smooth and unadorned, with a blend of wood and metal finishes.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full profile of the pistol, including the wooden grip, metallic barrel, hammer, and trigger guard.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the flared muzzle of the barrel and the octagonal shape of the opening.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the smooth wooden grip and the metallic components, with the hammer and frizzen visible.
description_view_3: Top-down view emphasizing the length and width of the barrel and the curvature of the grip.
attribute_grip_design: The grip is smoothly contoured with a gentle curve, crafted from polished wood that gives it a classic, elegant appearance. There are no visible finger grooves, checkering, or stippling, resulting in a sleek and simple surface. The wood grain is subtly visible, enhancing the traditional aesthetic. No logos, embellishments, or ergonomic features are present, emphasizing a utilitarian yet refined design.
attribute_trigger_guard_style: The trigger guard is metallic, slender, and gently curved, forming an oval loop around the trigger. It is relatively thin and unadorned, with no visible texturing, reinforcement, or undercuts. The design is simple and functional, lacking any squared-off edges or integrated accessories.
attribute_barrel_type: The barrel is short and flared at the muzzle, resembling a blunderbuss style. It has a smooth, metallic finish with no visible threading, porting, or external features such as compensators. The exterior is plain, with no markings or decorative elements.
attribute_slide_markings: There are no visible markings, engravings, or inscriptions on the slide or barrel. The surfaces are smooth and unadorned, with no manufacturer logos, model names, serial numbers, or decorative patterns present.
attribute_magazine_base_shape: The magazine base is not visible, as this is a single-shot, muzzle-loading pistol without a detachable magazine. The grip terminates in a rounded, smooth wooden end with no extensions, flares, or finger rests.
